title: RuleFile
description: A `rules/<lang>.yml` file: a target language plus its ordered strip rules.

Gate.description: Which drop flag, if any, gates this rule.

NthChildSimple.description: A string or number describing the indices of matching nodes in a list of siblings.

anyOf.description: A number indicating the precise element index
Functional notation like CSS's An + B

PatternStyle.description:
A String pattern will match one single AST node according to pattern syntax.
Or an object with field `context`, `selector` and optionally `strictness`.

context.description: The surrounding code that helps to resolve any ambiguity in the syntax.

selector.description: The sub-syntax node kind that is the actual matcher of the pattern.

strictness.description: Strictness of the pattern. More strict pattern matches fewer nodes.

Relation.description: A relational rule object, which is a Rule object with two additional fields stopBy and field.

all.description:
A list of sub rules and matches a node if all of sub rules match.
The meta variables of the matched node contain all variables from the sub-rules.

any.description:
A list of sub rules and matches a node if any of sub rules match.
The meta variables of the matched node only contain those of the matched sub-rule.

follows.description:
`follows` accepts a relational rule object.
the target node must appear after another node matching the `follows` sub-rule.

has.description:
`has` accepts a relational rule object.
the target node must has a descendant node matching the `has` sub-rule.

inside.description:
`inside` accepts a relational rule object.
the target node must appear inside of another node matching the `inside` sub-rule.

kind.description: The kind name of the node to match. You can look up code's kind names in playground.

matches.description:
A utility rule id or parameterized utility call object.
When multiple utility calls are present, they are combined with logical `all`.

not.description: A single sub-rule and matches a node if the sub rule does not match.

nthChild.description:
`nth_child` accepts number, string or object.
It specifies the position in nodes' sibling list.

pattern.description: A pattern string or a pattern object.

precedes.description:
`precedes` accepts a relational rule object.
the target node must appear before another node matching the `precedes` sub-rule.

range.description:
`range` accepts a range object.
the target node must exactly appear in the range.

regex.description: A Rust regular expression to match the node's text. https://docs.rs/regex/latest/regex/#syntax

RuleAction.description: What to do with each matched range.

RuleSpec.description: One strip rule: an ast-grep matcher plus what to do with each hit.

SerializableNthChild.description: `nthChild` accepts either a number, a string or an object.

ofRule.description: select the nth node that matches the rule, like CSS's of syntax

reverse.description: matches from the end instead like CSS's nth-last-child

SerializablePosition.description: Represents a zero-based character-wise position in a document

column.description: 0-based column number in the source code

line.description: 0-based line number in the source code

SerializableRange.description: Represents a position in source code using 0-based line and column numbers

end.description: end position in the source code

start.description: start position in the source code

SerializableRule.description:
A rule object to find matching AST nodes. We have three categories of rules in ast-grep.

* Atomic: the most basic rule to match AST. We have two variants: Pattern and Kind.

* Relational: filter matched target according to their position relative to other nodes.

* Composite: use logic operation all/any/not to compose the above rules to larger rules.

Every rule has it's unique name so we can combine several rules in one object.

all.description:
A list of sub rules and matches a node if all of sub rules match.
The meta variables of the matched node contain all variables from the sub-rules.

any.description:
A list of sub rules and matches a node if any of sub rules match.
The meta variables of the matched node only contain those of the matched sub-rule.

follows.description:
`follows` accepts a relational rule object.
the target node must appear after another node matching the `follows` sub-rule.

has.description:
`has` accepts a relational rule object.
the target node must has a descendant node matching the `has` sub-rule.

inside.description:
`inside` accepts a relational rule object.
the target node must appear inside of another node matching the `inside` sub-rule.

kind.description: The kind name of the node to match. You can look up code's kind names in playground.

matches.description:
A utility rule id or parameterized utility call object.
When multiple utility calls are present, they are combined with logical `all`.

not.description: A single sub-rule and matches a node if the sub rule does not match.

nthChild.description:
`nth_child` accepts number, string or object.
It specifies the position in nodes' sibling list.

pattern.description: A pattern string or a pattern object.

precedes.description:
`precedes` accepts a relational rule object.
the target node must appear before another node matching the `precedes` sub-rule.

range.description:
`range` accepts a range object.
the target node must exactly appear in the range.

regex.description: A Rust regular expression to match the node's text. https://docs.rs/regex/latest/regex/#syntax

SerializableStopBy.description: Control how the relational rule search should stop

oneOf.description: all nodes except source trivial nodes are matched.
similar to smart, but node kinds are ignored, only text is matched.

Threshold.description: Which configured byte threshold a match must clear to count.
